Neet 2026 -my first attempt 
Was excited,13hrs study for atleast first six months.
Then hit by health problems, burnout,nd then depression.
Screen time 10 hrs/per day.
Never talked to someone about that -just lying in good ,yes studying.

No words 🙏🏼🙏🏼🙏🏼

I passed out my 12th in 2024 and took 2 drops for the exam. So total 3 attempt in the neet. The reality is that the I never took seriously to anything and now I am currently 20. I was like the student who always says " kal ho jayega .so I  leave it and the kal has not come yet."
In the mean time the time is also near. The problem is that we are too obsessed with the things that sometimes we live in false world of expectations and security that we can do it. The most traumatic thing that happened to me is that in the pursuit of becoming the doctor I become the patient of porn addiction and depression. Sacrificing my health and sleep. 

Now I have chose to complete leave it and start a new chapter of life choosing pharma. Although it was not the end but still I have decided that I will attempt neet 2026 and 27 for the last time. But things I will approach differently. This drop year has taught me that I am a type of student who can do a lot when my environment is not so pressurizing and I will not sacrifice my mental health and physical health. The stress and the anxiety that I have taken in the pursuit of becoming a doctor has teached me a lot about how to live my life. So I can also say that this drop years also taught me many things that I carry forward in to my own life. And the most important lesson that I learnt is that - we definitely achieve goals but not sacrificing the things that give us happiness or helps us to uplift our moods. I know it is irrelevant to the things that great things require sacrifices but I have seen that only sacrificing the things and not doing anything or only studying has made me dumb in real life.

How can I stop burnout?
To avoid burnout, follow these tips:
1
Work with purpose.
2
Perform a job analysis, and eliminate or delegate unnecessary work.
3
Give to others.
4
Take control, and actively manage your time.
5
Get more exercise.
6
Learn how to manage stress.

What does burnout feel like?
Being burned out means feeling empty and mentally exhausted, devoid of motivation, and beyond caring. People experiencing burnout often don't see any hope of positive change in their situations.

Quotes to Get You Through Your 'Burnout'
“Don't say you don't have enough time. ...
“You've got to get up every morning with determination if you're going to go to bed with satisfaction.” – George Lorimer.
“Procrastination makes easy things hard, hard things harder.” – Mason Cooley.

Thought-Provoking Burnout Quotes
“Self-care is how you take your power back.” — ...
“I have a theory that burnout is about resentment. ...
“No beating yourself up. ...
“The No. ...
“Take rest. ...
“Being overwhelmed means that your life or work is overpowering you. ...
“Stress is the trash of modern life.

​ @sanjupandey6046  Firstly there should have been focus on proper distribution and proper skewing of doctors amongst population. Increasing population does not demand just reckless production of doctors. Even lacks of cases are still pending in the courts, does govt increase the mass production of Judges. Administrative services are paralyzed in state, does govt can ever plan the increment of number of seats in UPSC CSE. Are u aware that for just 75 post of junior residents in one of the govt medical institutions,more than 1000 candidates had applied. U have misdiagnosed the problem of country's health system as lack of doctors. It's more about lack of medical infrastructure,  improper and abandoned machines and instruments ,over interference of bureaucrats and Netas and finally insecurity of physical and mental abuse amongst doctors. "Quantity compromises the quality". Even after toiling more than 10 good years if a medico receives job insecurity and disappointments, then it leads to clinical depression. As far as working hrs are concerned, it will get even worsened as govt now will have confidence that if not u, then someone else will do the work, may be even at lower wages because they have produced a lots of "white coat labours". The apt example to quote is southern India, as lots of MBBS producing factories, leads to paying a minimum salary of just 30k-40k.

As an MBBS student, believe me, you don't need any "toxic-senior's" guidance to excel in MBBS.

Your professors know way more than them. Respect your seniors, but don't compromise your values. 

Those who disrespect you, stay away from them because they are showing their inner 'dirty' self. This might be the way their parents wouod have raised them. These poor non-sense rascals. 

Regarding studies, that was the thing of past where you had no YouTube and you were somewhat dependent on seniors. Now you can access literally everything on YouTube.

The way you try to make with the 'toxic' seniors, make a good bond with professors instead.

Now I don't mean, don't befriend with a senior. If you find a good one, excellent. But if not, still, no problem. Just take it easy and never compromise your values, no matter what.

And also one most important thing, when you become a 'senior', please don't be a toxic one. 

I literally hate such toxic, ego-centered, animal-like monsters from my heart. I give them no weight, yet I give them curse.

First off please only take this advice from her only if you're ready to face its consequences. First be humble then refute. Just to let you knoe things may not go as peacefully as things did in her case.
The entire concept of gaining mental fortitude by bullying is toxic af, but you also get to know your own attitude amidst the chaos. You can easily get a senior thrown outta college for bullying, but in the future when you r among a group of full fledged doctors, nurses and senior residents, you cannot just cut them off from your life, so you have to be humble and likeable and play along with some of their jokes initially if not worst case they will not pass by a chance to insult you and being insulted out in public in front of patients about your incompetence may not feel that good and if you speak up doesn't mean they won't insult you any more, you'll be overworked, throwed insults at as vengeance, and you'll have to face consequences and humiliation of creating uproar in a hospital as a doctor, and the seniors that you liked and trust will remain nothing but bystanders. So be humble and respectful unless they are taking it way too far is what I'd advice cuz no one wants to end up in a mess theydidn't sign up for.

I always believed this that the ragging/oppression happens because we tend to give in for it at some or the other level due to fear,pear pressure etc. If we stand for ourselves and take strong measures against such acts then such things can always be avoided. Thank you for the reconfirmation dude !
Hamre college (GMC-Shivpuri) mein staff ragging ko aise ingnore karta hai ki kya hi batao😂.......
in some colleges they will literally kill you if you retaliate against ragging, there have been incidents like this before

1. This culture is not very cool. I understand but, people who normally join mbbs are between the age group of 17-20 yrs , which I believe is a very fragile age group. You stood up for urself . Thats good because you could identify the threat in that interaction and you clearly tackled it very maturely.
But a student who is not as mature/confident as you are since you have had your experience in dealing with people , right. They fall for it and thats it the person is scarred and he is on the road of becoming worse. This is a case with 50 percent of people. But its not like that every time.

2. Let me tell you What i have learned from my mbbs experience. Seniors throw shit. A lot of shit. I know. I have faced a hell lot more than this, but few of my seniors also showed us the kindness and care we deserved and that is the part bullies miss. They don’t make up for it. They just go overboard with it. Thats not how it should be. Because we obeyed their orders and gave ourselves away, is this the reason for their kindness? I guess, No.
This kind of interaction shows a lot about you. how u deal with uncertainties, insults, and all the shit you can imagine. This profession is not a fairy tale. What about those senior doctors/consultants/ professors who would question your competence, skills, behaviour in a very derogatory way which u cant even imagine of. They will not care about it. You will have immense sense of incompetence. And You wont be able to say even a single word of self defence to him/her. You cant deny this fact that these people exist. They do and this does not mean they are bad. Im not saying become like them but ất least learn to tolerate to a certain extent.
Thats when this habit of taking shit comes into play because you know that Dr is just like a senior you had in your med school. 

3. Im not saying i support extreme bullying or extreme ragging but the culture of intolerance is just not going to help everybody. People like you can easily deal with it and will continue to deal with it because again you had your experience. And some have the intrinsic ability to do it. Thats great. 
Let kids oppose unjust things but don’t make them intolerant. It would harm their future. 
I have literally seen few of my colleagues going into depression post mbbs because they don’t feel like they know how to deal with this toxicity. This toxicity is unfortunately inevitable. At least  make yourself strong. And so strong that you can identify what are the limits of your tolerance. And when you have to go against it. 
This profession demands grit and perseverance. There is long academic career ahead of you. Good luck for that.

Thank you and have a nice day!
